message:
feat(mvcc): track active snapshot refcounts for correct GC horizon caching

Refactor the GC horizon tracking in the MVCC transaction manager to
correctly handle duplicate snapshot_high values from concurrent readers.

Previously, releasing one transaction with a given snapshot_high would
clear the cached horizon even if other active transactions still held
the same snapshot. This could allow premature garbage collection of
pages still visible to live readers.

Changes:
- Add `active_snapshot_counts: BTreeMap<CommitSeq, usize>` to
  TransactionManager for reference-counting active snapshot_high values
- Extract `increment_active_snapshot_refcount()`,
  `decrement_active_snapshot_refcount()`, and
  `active_snapshot_horizon_raw()` helpers
- `cached_gc_horizon()` now returns the minimum key from the refcount
  map (the oldest still-active snapshot)
- Horizon only clears when the LAST transaction at a given snapshot_high
  releases, not the first

Add two new tests:
- `test_cached_gc_horizon_retains_duplicate_snapshot_highs_until_last_release`:
  verifies horizon stays pinned when multiple txns share the same snapshot
- `test_cached_gc_horizon_advances_to_next_snapshot_without_full_clear`:
  verifies releasing the oldest snapshot advances horizon to the next one

Beads issue tracker metadata updated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-db300.3.5.1","title":"C5.1: define the advanced durability-lane and group-commit design space","description":"## Goal
Define the advanced durability-lane and group-commit design space without committing to unnecessary complexity.

## Checklist
1. Define the candidate durability-lane structures, including per-worker logs or equivalent batched durability paths.
2. Make epoch, flush, client-notification, backpressure, and memory-budget rules explicit.
3. Keep rollback to the simpler batch-publish design explicit from the start.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Candidate durability-lane structures and their epoch or flush semantics are explicit.
- Client notification, backpressure, and memory-budget rules are named.
- Rollback to the simpler batch-publish path remains explicit.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Candidate durability-lane structures and their epoch or flush semantics are explicit. 2. Client notification, backpressure, and memory-budget rules are named. 3. Rollback to the simpler batch-publish path remains explicit.","notes":"This bead guards against inventing advanced durability machinery without a clean contract.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T17:04:09.332508231Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:35:38.808133944Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["db300-program","db300-track-c","durability","group-commit","parallelism","perf","track-c","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:59Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:59Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:59Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.3.5.2","title":"C5.2: prototype one advanced durability path only if the design-space evidence justifies it","description":"## Goal
Prototype at most one advanced durability path only if the design-space evidence justifies it.

## Checklist
1. Prototype at most one advanced durability-lane design with bounded queueing and observability hooks.
2. Keep publish-path rollback and operator disablement straightforward.
3. Avoid treating the prototype as mandatory if the simpler batch path remains better.

## Acceptance Criteria
- At most one advanced durability path is prototyped.
- The prototype keeps queueing bounded, observable, and easy to disable.
- The work remains optional rather than mandatory.","acceptance_criteria":"1. At most one advanced durability path is prototyped. 2. The prototype keeps queueing bounded, observable, and easy to disable. 3. The work is treated as optional rather than mandatory.","notes":"This is intentionally downstream of the simpler C1/C2 path and should stay that way.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T17:04:24.535913059Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:35:41.885501293Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["db300-program","db300-track-c","durability","group-commit","parallelism","perf","track-c","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:59Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:59Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.3.5.3","title":"C5.3: verify crash recovery benchmark impact and go-no-go for advanced durability lanes","description":"## Goal
Verify crash recovery and benchmark impact for the advanced durability path and produce a clear go/no-go outcome.

## Checklist
1. Verify crash-recovery and durability semantics under the advanced path.
2. Benchmark queueing delay, durability lag, and throughput against the simpler C1/C2 baseline.
3. Produce a go-no-go decision record instead of letting the advanced path linger by default.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Crash-recovery and durability semantics are validated for the advanced path.
- Queueing delay, durability lag, and throughput are compared against the simpler baseline.
- The outcome is a go-no-go decision record rather than an indefinite experiment.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Crash-recovery and durability semantics are validated for the advanced path. 2. Queueing delay, durability lag, and throughput are compared against the simpler baseline. 3. The outcome is a go-no-go decision record rather than an indefinite experiment.","notes":"Advanced durability lanes are only justified if they clearly beat the simpler design without semantic damage.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T17:04:39.561998121Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:35:44.993964391Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["db300-program","db300-track-c","durability","evidence","group-commit","track-c","verification","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:59Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:59Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:59Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-db300.3.6","title":"C6: remove page-1 allocator and freelist conflict surface from logically disjoint writers","description":"## Goal
Eliminate the remaining page-1/freelist conflict surface that still makes logically disjoint writers collide in the canonical `commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys` benchmark. The current profiler evidence shows `allocate_page` and `free_page` still track `PageNumber::ONE` as a conflict-only page, so writers that should be independent converge on a shared hotspot before commit.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The conditions that truly require page-1/freelist conflict tracking are explicit and tested.
- Logically disjoint insert workloads no longer collide on `PageNumber::ONE` unless correctness requires it.
- Canonical `commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys` reruns show materially lower retry/abort pressure and better throughput.
- Targeted unit tests and canonical-matrix e2e coverage prove correctness and measure the improvement.
- Structured profiling artifacts tie before/after results to the exact workload, fixture, and concurrency cells.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T22:40:03.126257608Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T22:40:03.126257608Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conflict-topology","db300-program","db300-track-c","hot-path","pager","perf","track-c","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T22:40:03.126257608Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-db300.3.7","title":"Eliminate residual page-1 conflict-only tracking from disjoint writer path","description":"## Goal
Stop forcing `PageNumber::ONE` into the MVCC write-conflict surface for logically disjoint insert/free workloads unless page-1 metadata is semantically changed. The current evidence pack for `commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys c8` shows catastrophic slowdown while the hottest path is `SharedTxnPageIo::write_page`, and the allocate/free path still calls `track_concurrent_conflict_only_page(..., PageNumber::ONE, ...)` in `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs` before pager allocation/free completes.

## Evidence
- `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs:1308` tracks `PageNumber::ONE` on `allocate_page`.
- `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs:1374` tracks `PageNumber::ONE` on `free_page`.
- `artifacts/perf/20260313_deep_profile/disjoint_c8_fsqlite_mvcc.perfreport.txt` shows ~95% of sampled cycles in `SharedTxnPageIo::write_page` with retry/conflict machinery visible.
- `artifacts/perf/20260313_deep_profile/disjoint_c8_fsqlite_mvcc.stdout.json` shows ~22.3 ops/s, 144 retries, and 35.9s wall time on the disjoint c8 cell.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. The exact semantic conditions under which page-1 must enter the conflict surface are explicit and tested.
2. Disjoint insert/free paths no longer create false page-1 conflicts when page-1 metadata is not actually mutated.
3. Targeted regression tests cover allocate/free success, rollback, and crash-safe restoration semantics.
4. The canonical `commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys` hot cell is re-profiled with before/after artifacts showing whether retries, instructions/op, and throughput improve.
5. Any remaining page-1 conflict cases are documented with rationale rather than hidden inside generic helper calls.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-13T22:40:24.407945139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T22:40:24.407945139Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conflict-topology","db300-program","db300-track-c","hot-path","mvcc","pager","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.7","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T22:40:24.407945139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.4","title":"Track D: VDBE, decode, and allocator hot-path reduction","description":"## Goal
This track targets the engine work still burning cycles even when no one is waiting. Sampled profiles showed quicksort, memmove, malloc/free, and parser/AST churn in the hot set. That means even after synchronization is reduced, FrankenSQLite will still lag unless decode, materialization, and allocation overhead are cut sharply.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The dominant non-wait CPU hotspots are explicitly measured.
- Follow-on beads remove at least one class of avoidable clone/allocation/copy churn.
- Results are measured in both MVCC and forced single-writer modes.","design":"- Produce opcode- and type-level evidence before changing internals blindly.
- Prefer reuse, arenas, and scratch-space strategies over repeated allocate/copy/free cycles.
- Attack clone/drop churn in prepared-program and row-materialization paths.
- Reduce memory movement in record decode and B-Tree cell assembly, potentially with SIMD-safe helpers when the data layout supports it.","acceptance_criteria":"- The dominant non-wait CPU hotspots are explicitly measured.
- Follow-on beads remove at least one class of avoidable clone/allocation/copy churn.
- Results are measured in both MVCC and forced single-writer modes.","notes":"This track is about reclaiming cycles after the synchronization tax is lowered. The purpose is not a vague cleanup pass; it is a targeted campaign against record decode, row materialization, parser/AST churn, and allocator pressure that is currently showing up in perf samples.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:46:45.246276448Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:55:31.330414182Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["copy-reduction","db300-program","db300-track-d","decoding","execution-engine","hot-path","memory-management","perf","track-d","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.4","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:59Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.4","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:59Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.4","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:59Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.4","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:59Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.4.1","title":"D1: produce opcode-level and type-level hot-path profiles on real fixtures","description":"## Goal
Produce an opcode- and type-level profile for the mixed_read_write hot path on the real Beads fixtures. The project already knows there is non-wait CPU cost in sort/memmove/malloc/parser churn, but this bead should localize that cost to concrete execution-engine responsibilities rather than a generic flamegraph blob.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The hot path is broken down by opcode or execution subsystem with evidence.
- Record decode, row materialization, parser/AST churn, and allocator pressure are quantified well enough to rank.
- The resulting ranking is actionable for D2-D4.
- The scripts, harness changes, or reports in this bead emit structured logs and artifact manifests rich enough to reproduce results without reconstructing the workflow by hand.","acceptance_criteria":"- The hot path is broken down by opcode or execution subsystem with evidence.
- Record decode, row materialization, parser/AST churn, and allocator pressure are quantified well enough to rank.
- The resulting ranking is actionable for D2-D4.
- The scripts, harness changes, or reports in this bead emit structured logs and artifact manifests rich enough to reproduce results without reconstructing the workflow by hand.","notes":"This bead is the CPU-side counterpart to B1. B1 explains where time is lost to waiting; D1 explains where time is lost to useful-but-too-expensive engine work.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:58:41.549202059Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-12T17:32:27.303900006Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T17:32:27.302267291Z","close_reason":"Canonical MVCC smoke campaign now completes end-to-end with opcode/subsystem packs, quantified hotspot rankings, finalized command-pack handoff, and the connection.rs runtime panic removed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["db300-program","db300-track-d","decoding","execution-engine","perf","profiling","track-d","vdbe"],"comments":[{"id":2252,"issue_id":"bd-db300.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"AmberOx update: inline hot-path profile capture now threads the workload snapshot through the e2e executor, hot-path tests pass, and remaining clippy reds are unrelated pre-existing lint debt in realdb_e2e / broader workspace surfaces. Working in crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/perf_runner.rs to emit a structured hotspot-to-bead ranking artifact for D2-D4 follow-on work; avoiding engine.rs and CLI surfaces already claimed by other agents.","created_at":"2026-03-10T17:03:20Z"},{"id":2254,"issue_id":"bd-db300.4.1","author":"Codex","text":"Clean note: continuing the D1 packaging/handoff slice in crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/perf_runner.rs. This change emits a structured actionable_ranking.json artifact that maps measured hotspots to downstream Track D beads (D2-D4) so follow-on work can pick up directly from the profile output. Avoiding engine.rs and CLI surfaces already claimed by other agents.","created_at":"2026-03-10T17:08:14Z"},{"id":2255,"issue_id":"bd-db300.4.1","author":"Codex","text":"Progress update: rewrote scripts/verify_bd_db300_4_1_hot_path_profiles.sh to drive the current realdb-e2e hot-profile path via rch instead of archived perf.data synthesis. The script now captures per-run profile.json/actionable_ranking.json/manifest.json bundles under artifacts/perf/bd-db300.4.1/inline/runs and aggregates them into bead-level scenario_profiles.json, actionable_ranking.json, benchmark_context.json, summary.md, and report.json. Running a filtered smoke pass now against frankensqlite_beads in MVCC mode to validate the new workflow end to end before widening to all fixtures and both modes.","created_at":"2026-03-10T17:11:54Z"},{"id":2256,"issue_id":"bd-db300.4.1","author":"Codex","text":"Verification: rustfmt completed on crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/perf_runner.rs and remote verification passed via rch: env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/var/tmp/rch_target_bd_db300_4_1_rank_2 cargo test -p fsqlite-e2e hot_path_profile_smoke_writes_artifacts -- --nocapture. This should make D2-D4 ranking evidence-backed once the parent bead is stitched together.","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:04:41Z"},{"id":2272,"issue_id":"bd-db300.4.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Tracker reconciliation note: command-pack artifact handoff was added to scripts/verify_bd_db300_4_1_hot_path_profiles.sh, and crates/fsqlite-core/src/remote_effects.rs was updated for the current asupersync cancel_entry(entry_id, now) API so the D1 smoke path compiles again.
